title: Offer Evaluation, Compensation, Negotiation, and Decision Quality · Hiring Scenario Checkpoint
summary: A hiring panel compares near-miss decisions for offer evaluation, compensation, negotiation, and decision quality and accepts only role-relevant, truthful, accessible, privacy-preserving, and independently defensible evidence. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for evidence-based offer decision model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Request and verify a written offer, role, level, reporting line, location, schedule, start, compensation, benefits, contingencies, and decision date.
Compare base, variable pay, equity uncertainty, benefits, leave, hours, commute, equipment, learning, stability, risk, and personal constraints.
Build a source-dated occupation, location, level, and responsibility range while separating public data from this employer decision.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# job-search cumulative learner-controlled career campaign
# Browser labs review fictional evidence contracts only. They never contact people,
# submit applications, access accounts, expose personal data, negotiate, or accept offers.
workspace: career
candidate-id: fictional-learner
target-role: [replace with role family, work outcomes, and bounded level]
jurisdiction: [replace with location and current official guidance boundary]
audience: [replace with reviewer need, time, format, and access context]
claim: [replace with one truthful role-relevant behavior claim]
evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, observation, changed case, contribution, and limit]
process-state: [replace with discover, investigate, apply, interview, offer, close, or archive]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
privacy-consent: [replace with purpose, minimization, permission, retention, deletion, and safe contact]
decision: [replace with learner-owned next decision and stop rule]
transfer-boundary: learner-approved external actions only
review:

steps.title: Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Written Offer
steps.instruction:
Order the Written Offer evidence cycle for this quiz task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Written Offer.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Written Offer assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Written Offer, keep Written Offer connected to mechanically reviewable target-role, truthful-claim, contribution, accessibility, privacy, consent, process-state, changed-candidate, failure, repair, decision, and transfer evidence.

content.text: A verbal offer creates urgency while level, manager, total compensation, contingencies, schedule, intellectual property terms, and decision time remain unclear. The quiz variation 4 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Request and verify a written offer, role, level, reporting line, location, schedule, start, compensation, benefits, contingencies, and decision date.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Written Offer has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

Competency probe: Request and verify a written offer, role, level, reporting line, location, schedule, start, compensation, benefits, contingencies, and decision date.

options.text: Run the boundary case, inspect the actual mechanism, then accept or repair the claim.

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Decision Record
steps.instruction:
Write the quiz evidence note for Decision Record: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A verbal offer creates urgency while level, manager, total compensation, contingencies, schedule, intellectual property terms, and decision time remain unclear. The quiz variation 6 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Document benefits, risks, unknowns, pressure, advice boundaries, decline conditions, acceptance terms, and the learner-owned reasoned decision.

Evidence target: Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Decision Record.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Decision Record, keep Decision Record connected to mechanically reviewable target-role, truthful-claim, contribution, accessibility, privacy, consent, process-state, changed-candidate, failure, repair, decision, and transfer evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Document benefits, risks, unknowns, pressure, advice boundaries, decline conditions, acceptance terms, and the learner-owned reasoned decision.
